strategic_offerings.label: Compute Sovereignty Brief
strategic_offerings.purpose: Deliver a scoped, governed synthesis of layered compute dependency exposure for a declared entity, sector, or deployment context.
suitable_when: An executive or strategy team needs a bounded read of dependency exposure before a major decision
The question is defined by sovereignty-stack layers, time horizon, and geographic scope
The client accepts that conclusions follow evidence quality and may remain partial
not_suitable_when: The client requires a sovereignty score, ranking, or predetermined verdict
The request seeks investment, legal, procurement, or national-security advice
The client expects guaranteed outcomes, certification, or vendor comparison
required_inputs: Decision context and intended use of the work
Declared time horizon and geographic scope
Known constraints, exclusions, and confidentiality boundaries
strategic_offerings.deliverable_boundary: A dated brief that separates evidence, analysis, confidence, unknowns, and limitations within the declared scope. It may describe exposure types and dependency conditions; it does not assign sovereignty or non-sovereignty.
strategic_offerings.evidence_basis: Governed source registry records, primary attributable evidence, and claim review under SOURCE_POLICY.md and CLAIM_POLICY.md. Insufficient evidence produces no operational finding.
trust_constraints: Public and private work follow the same source and claim standards
Commercial relationships must be disclosed where material
No predetermined conclusion may be purchased
Material conflicts must be disclosed rather than averaged away

strategic_offerings.label: Chip Dependency Audit
strategic_offerings.purpose: Review layered dependency exposure against the Chip Dependency Map model for a declared scope, producing governed dependency-record outputs where evidence permits.
suitable_when: A team needs structured dependency exposure mapped to the sovereignty stack
The scope, layers, and evidence base can be declared before work begins
The client accepts audit outputs as governed records, not certifications
not_suitable_when: The client requires certification of independence, resilience, compliance, or readiness
The request depends on unavailable, stale, or unattributable evidence
The client expects a universal score or comparative league table
required_inputs: Layers and exposure types under review
Available evidence, data access boundaries, and known gaps
strategic_offerings.deliverable_boundary: Scoped dependency records, diagnostic responses, confidence labels, limitations, and as-of dates where evidence supports them. The audit reveals exposure; it does not certify capability, access, or deployment success.
strategic_offerings.evidence_basis: Chip Dependency Map protocol, dependency record schema, registered sources, and claim-specific review. Diagnostic questions are evidence-led; missing evidence is recorded as insufficient rather than weakness.
trust_constraints: Audit scope and evidence limits are stated before delivery
No question response is treated as a deployment or purchase recommendation
Registry presence does not automatically create a finding
Conflicts and stale evidence require visible disclosure

strategic_offerings.label: Premium Reference Dossier
strategic_offerings.purpose: Produce a deep, governed reference dossier on a declared topic within the compute sovereignty ontology for durable institutional use.
suitable_when: A research, policy, or strategy organization needs a maintained reference artifact
The topic can be bounded by layers, geography, and time
The client values source transparency and methodological limits over headline conclusions
not_suitable_when: The client requires product reviews, vendor rankings, or affiliate-style recommendations
The topic depends on speculative supply, capacity, or market-share narratives without evidence
The client expects investment advice or procurement direction
required_inputs: Target audience and retention or licensing intent
Required layers, geographies, and temporal scope
strategic_offerings.deliverable_boundary: A structured reference dossier with sourced claims, confidence labels, limitations, registry references, and as-of dating. It extends public reference value; it does not become a ranking, purchase recommendation, or investment brief.
strategic_offerings.evidence_basis: Source registry, primary evidence hierarchy, Compute Sovereignty Standard, and explicit separation of verified fact, attributed statement, estimate, analysis, and unknown.
trust_constraints: Dossier claims remain bounded by evidence and scope
Sponsored or commercial influence must not alter source selection
Corrections follow visible governance when material errors are found
Public excerpts must preserve the same claim standards as private work

strategic_offerings.label: Ontology and Methodology Licensing
strategic_offerings.purpose: License governed use of the compute sovereignty ontology, Chip Dependency Map methodology, standards, or reference structures under written terms.
suitable_when: An institution wants to apply the ontology or methodology inside its own governed analysis
The use case requires documented boundaries, attribution, and update expectations
The licensee accepts that licensing does not confer official accreditation
not_suitable_when: The request seeks resale of rankings, scores, or ungoverned derivative products
The licensee requires implied government, industry, or academic endorsement
The use case depends on stripping evidence, confidence, or limitation requirements
required_inputs: Intended use case and deployment context
Requested components: ontology, methodology, standard, or model elements
Geographic scope, audience, and modification requirements
strategic_offerings.deliverable_boundary: A written licensing scope covering permitted use, required attribution, evidence standards, prohibited derivatives, and update boundaries. Licensing grants structured reference rights; it does not certify the licensee's outputs or confer sovereignty judgments.
strategic_offerings.evidence_basis: Published foundation documents, Compute Sovereignty Standard, Chip Dependency Map protocol, and source registry governance. Licensed use must preserve claim classes, confidence labels, and limitation disclosure.
trust_constraints: License terms preserve neutrality and evidence requirements
Derivative products must not misrepresent governed outputs as official findings
Material changes to licensed methodology require documented agreement
Public references to the asset must not imply purchased conclusions

strategic_offerings.label: Strategic Acquisition Inquiry
strategic_offerings.purpose: Provide a serious, bounded channel for institutions evaluating strategic interest in the asset, ontology, reference library, or governed engagement pathways.
suitable_when: A buyer or institution is evaluating strategic fit, durability, and governance
The inquiry concerns the asset, methodology, or reference surface rather than a predetermined operational verdict
The party accepts that public reference value and private analysis follow the same standards
not_suitable_when: The inquiry seeks a packaged sovereignty verdict on a country, company, or chip
The party expects revenue projections, price quotes, or checkout through the public site
The contact is primarily seeking affiliate, advertising, or sponsored-content placement
required_inputs: Strategic interest: asset, ontology, dossiers, audits, or licensing
Time horizon and intended post-acquisition use
strategic_offerings.deliverable_boundary: A qualified strategic conversation covering asset scope, governance, evidence posture, commercial boundaries, and fit. It does not provide acquisition recommendations, valuations, or guarantees about future performance.
strategic_offerings.evidence_basis: Public foundation documents, governed model and registry assets, decision log, monetization boundary, and disclosed limitations of the current reference surface.
trust_constraints: Strategic discussions preserve reference independence
No public claim of official accreditation or government endorsement
Acquisition interest does not alter public findings or source posture
Serious inquiries only; vague or promotional contacts may be declined
